  explicit implementation tags

docs: fix masternode collateral amounts in CLAUDE.md

Per protocol spec §5.2:
- Free: 0 TIME (was incorrectly implied as 1,000)
- Bronze: 1,000 TIME (was 10,000)
- Silver: 10,000 TIME (was 100,000)
- Gold: 100,000 TIME (was 1,000,000)

Also add sampling weights (Free=1, Bronze=10, Silver=100, Gold=1,000)
which are the actual values used for TimeVote stake weighting.

commit.message:
Bug fix for payment portal (#118)

* feat(fees): Implement Comprehensive Fee Management and Payment Roster System (#4)

* fix: improve student CSV bulk upload and suffix handling

Validates file type is CSV and ensures names with suffixes are correctly identified during the import process.

* fix: implement shortName and composite indices for program filtering

Switches program references to shortName and adds composite indexing to improve filter query speeds.

* fix: resolve pagination bugs and support UTF-8 characters in imports

Fixes filtering/pagination logic, adds database indices for the attendees list, and ensures "ñ" is correctly handled during member creation/import.

* fix: resolve QUIC protocol errors with batching and progress UI

Fixes connection crashes on 9,000+ student imports by chunking Firestore writes into batches of 400 and introducing a slight network delay between commits. Also upgrades the bulk import dialog with a real-time progress bar and interaction locks to prevent accidental user cancellation.

* fix: build error in faculty and program type

* fix: ensures add event modal is scrollable

* Update src/firebase/bulkImport.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: pr review fixes

* fix: build issue

* feat(fees): implement modular fee generation with confirmation flow

- Create [useFeeGeneration] hook to encapsulate form state and generation logic
- Refactor `AddFeeDialog` into a lean UI component using the custom hook
- Implement [ConfirmDialog] for verifying batch fee generation
- Create [FeesPage] as the central management entry point
- Fix TypeScript type mismatch in `FeeGenerationSchema` for `isRequiredForClearance`
- Resolve field name inconsistency between schema and form fields

* feat(fees): add fee list UI and refactor fee generation logic

Implemented the initial Fee List interface with search and pagination, refactored the Fees data model for consistency, and modularized the fee generation process.

* feat(fees): implement fees roster UI and data fetching logic

- Add `fetchFeeRoster` and `fetchPaymentLogs` to Firebase service for data retrieval
- Create comprehensive Fees Roster UI components:
  - `FeesRosterPage` & `FeesRosterContent`: Main layout and state management
  - `AllStudentsDisplay` & `AllStudentsView`: Roster visualization and student listings
  - `StatCards`: Key metrics display
  - `SearchFilterBar`: Advanced filtering controls
  - `ManualPaymentDialog` & `PaymentDetailDialog`: Payment management interfaces
  - `SubmissionView` & `PaymentDisplay`: Student-specific fee status and logs
- Update `types.ts` and hooks (`useFeeList`, `useFeesRoster`) to support new fetching logic
- Define status configurations and schemas for consistent payment state management

* feat(fees): implement manual payment handling and roster status management

Key changes included in this update:
- Integrated [useFeeAction] hook to provide approvePayment, rejjectPayment, and addManualPayment functionalities.
- Refactored FeesRosterContent and FeesRosterPage to utilize these actions and better manage student payment states.
- Standardized the Row data type across [AllStudentsView] and `AllStudentsDisplay` to handle `Partial<Member>` data safely.
- Updated ManualPaymentDialog to correctly reference student records and trigger manual payment logging.
- Adjusted Firebase utility functions to point to the `payment_history` subcollection and use `paid_at` for ordering.
- Added a new "Paid" status configuration to `statusConfig.ts` for consistent UI feedback.

* feat(fees): refactor payment logs to camelCase and implement auto-refresh for actions

- Refactor [PaymentLog] interface fields from snake_case to camelCase for consistency.
- Implement [approvePaymentTransaction] and [rejectPaymentTransaction] with proper transaction handling.
- Rename `payment_history` subcollection to `paymentHistory` in Firestore operations.
- Enhance [useFeeAction] hook with an `onSuccess` callback to trigger local data synchronization.
- Update [useFeesRoster] hook to provide a `refetchStudentRow` function for targeted data refresh.
- Update UI components (`PaymentDisplay`, [PaymentDetailDialog]), `AllStudentsDisplay`) to support the new camelCase field names and subcollection structure.
- Wire up auto-refresh in [FeesRosterPage] to ensure the UI updates immediately after approving, rejecting, or recording a payment.

* feat(fees): finalize fee management and roster features

- Refactored fee list UI logic into a dedicated `useFeeListUI` hook for better state management.
- Implemented type-based filtering in the fee list view using the new `SearchFilterFee` component.
- Improved `PaymentDetailDialog` to display verification metadata (verified by and verified at).
- Standardized semester and academic year display formats across the fees roster and list views.
- Updated `AggregatedFee` type definition and moved computation logic to `useFeeList` hook.
- Cleaned up debug console logs and improved code organization.

* feat: Improve clearance status tracking and unify payment logic

This commit introduces significant improvements to how student clearance statuses are tracked and managed, alongside a major refactoring to unify the payment handling logic for both fees and fines.

Key Changes:
- **Clearance Status Improvements**:
  - Restructured the `blockingItems` within the `clearanceStatus` document to track individual fine items (using a unique reference ID) rather than just the aggregate fine document. This allows for more granular tracking and visualization of what exactly is blocking a student's clearance.
  - Updated `generateFinesOnEvent` to update the student's clearance document with the specific fine item ID, amount, and status ("unpaid") when a new fine is generated.
  - Refined `fetchClearanceDocuments` and related utilities to handle this new granular structure.
- **Unified Payment Backend**:
  - Centralized the backend payment history logic in `paymentHistory.ts`. Functions like `addOfflineFinesPayment` were renamed and refactored to generic `addOfflinePayment` functions that support both `FEES` and `FINES` contexts.
  - Updated `clearance.ts` to use these centralized verification and rejection functions, ensuring consistent behavior when acknowledging or rejecting payments from the clearance management interface.
  - Refactored `fees.ts` to remove redundant payment transaction functions in favor of the unified backend.
- **UI Enhancements**:
  - Reorganized the Payments and Clearance interfaces.
  - Added new components: `ClearanceCard`, `ClearanceTable`, and `LogManualPaymentDialog` for better data presentation.
  - Split the Payments page into modular `SubmissionsTab` and `UnpaidTab` components, with corresponding Card and Table views for each.
- **TypeScript & Type Definitions**: Updated `PaymentType` and `ClearanceStatus` related interfaces in `types.ts` to reflect the new data structures.

These changes ensure that the clearance system accurately reflects real-time financial obligations and simplifies the codebase by reusing the same robust payment processing logic for all transaction types.

* style(clearance): enhance UI for manual payment logging dialog

This commit improves the user interface of the `LogManualPaymentDialog` by organizing unsettled items into distinct, intuitive groups (Fees, Fines, Other Requirements) and enhancing the visual hierarchy of the selection process.

Key Changes:
- **Grouped Items**: Unsettled items are now grouped by their type (`fees`, `fines`, `other`), making it easier for admins to identify what the student is paying for.
- **Improved List Items**:
  - Added specific icons (`CreditCard` for fees, `AlertCircle` for fines) to distinguish item types visually.
  - Enhanced the styling of individual selectable items, including dynamic border and background colors based on the item type (e.g., green accents for fees, red accents for fines).
- **Sub-Totals Breakdown**: The selection summary now displays a detailed breakdown of the total amount selected, showing separate sub-totals for Fees, Fines, and Other items, giving admins a clearer picture of the transaction composition before logging it.
- **Styling Tweaks**: Removed obsolete borders and utilized Tailwind's `cn` utility for cleaner class merging and state-based styling.

refactor: migrate from SDL2 to raylib with DoD improvements (#10)

* refactor: migrate from SDL2 to raylib with DoD improvements

- Replace SDL2/SDL2_ttf with raylib for rendering, input, and windowing
- Rename Color → DrawColor and Gesture → SwipeGesture to avoid raylib conflicts
- Remove SDL_Renderer* from render_system and text_draw signatures
- Add exe-relative font path via GetApplicationDirectory()
- Track InputSource (Mouse/Touch) to prevent hybrid device conflicts
- Edge-trigger focus-loss auto-pause (was_focused in InputState, not static)
- Remove const_cast in pick_font; return const Font& instead
- Eliminate all try_get in collision_system via per-archetype views
- Remove ParticleData::start_size; compute shrink at render time from Lifetime
- Update CMakeLists.txt, vcpkg.json, build.sh, and all tests/benchmarks

commit.message:
feat: stable YouTube streaming with cross-platform mic input (#16)

* feat: stable YouTube streaming with cross-platform mic input

- Replace frame-rate-limiting FramePacer with a dedicated StreamingThread
  that uses coarse sleep + spin-wait for sub-millisecond timing accuracy,
  decoupling display loop speed from stream delivery entirely
- Add AudioCapture class: captures system default mic via sounddevice and
  serves raw PCM over a local TCP socket that ffmpeg reads from — works on
  Windows, macOS, and Linux without hardcoding any device names
- Fall back to platform-native audio (avfoundation/pulse/dshow) if
  sounddevice is not installed, with a clear install message
- Add -tune zerolatency to cut encoder-side latency to near zero
- Add -thread_queue_size 512 and -rtbufsize 100M to prevent silent audio
  packet drops when mixing pipe video with live audio capture
- Fix Python-level pipe buffering (bufsize=0 + explicit flush) that was
  causing frames to arrive at ffmpeg in large bursts
- Add -flvflags no_duration_filesize to avoid FLV metadata buffering
- Reduce bufsize to 2x bitrate for tighter CBR (was 6x)

- Internal Flow2Categorical for discretized local search with sphere sampling
- Internal SearchThread for step adaptation and cost tracking

cost projection customization, step sizing, convergence, backward
compatibility, determinism, categorical discretization, and factory pattern.

- Compute fairness_map from on-chain blocks_without_reward history
  (deterministic across all validators, no gossip dependency)
- After amount tolerance check, verify the paid wallet matches the
  expected fairness-rotation winner (highest blocks_without_reward/10,
  address-ascending tie-break)
- Bitmap drift guard: only enforce when actual winner appears in our
  decoded bitmap nodes, preventing false positives from Free-tier churn
- UTXO-enforcement redirect accepted when collateral_utxo_tier_map
  confirms the actual payout address belongs to the correct tier
- Tied candidates (same fairness_bonus) are all accepted as valid

Without this check a modified producer could self-award the Silver,
Bronze, or Gold pool every block. All validator nodes accepted the
block because the total amount per tier was correct. Confirmed active
exploitation observed on mainnet (blocks 1314, 1317-1325).
